mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions signals an impending economic contraction, triggering corporate earnings revisions downward and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC drops below -0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of housing starts and consumer credit growth captures aggregate demand momentum; when both series expand simultaneously, it confirms robust consumption spending that lifts equity valuations over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (HOUST × RSAFS) shows no statistically significant predictive power (p > 0.10) for 20-day forward returns, or the joint signal produces negative returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in industrial production relative to demand indicators signals improving supply chain efficiency and margin recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as corporate profitability improves.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns in periods of rising INDPRO are not statistically different from flat periods (t-test p > 0.05), or the rolling Rank IC fails to exceed 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon as equity markets price in stronger near-term earnings.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between quarterly GDP growth surprises and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.10 over a 5-year window, indicating no predictive linkage.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds a restrictive threshold, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon until policy eases.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of observations where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: The divergence between nonfarm payrolls growth and the unemployment rate captures labor market tightness; when payroll growth accelerates while unemployment remains stable, it signals wage-push inflation that forces rate hikes and depresses equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (PAYEMS growth × UNRATE level) shows no significant predictive power for 60-day forward returns (p > 0.10), or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances indicate household leverage expansion and consumption strength, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in > 55% of instances where RSAFS rises while CPIAUCSL remains below 3.5%, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 2-year rolling window.
